Most teams don’t realize they’re handling data that can’t live in a standard Microsoft 365 environment.  And by the time a compliance requirement or contract review flags it… the timeline is already tight. 😬  Companies that handle controlled unclassified information \(CUI\) or International Traffic in Arms Regulations \(ITAR\) should be aware when GCC High is required. Those that handle federal contract information \(FCI\) can still use GCC.  This guide breaks down: ♦️ What GCC High is ♦️ Who needs it ♦️ How you can get GCCH licenses ♦️ Difference between M365 GCC vs. GCC High  If your team is unsure whether GCC High applies to you, a quick review now can save major stress later on. Cybersecurity usually isn’t ignored.  It just doesn’t always rise to the top when everything else feels more immediate.  As teams grow, systems stack up, and responsibilities shift, risk exposure can increase quietly... often without clear visibility. Small gaps don’t always look urgent at the moment, but they tend to compound over time
Cybersecurity added later usually costs more, because unmanaged risk compounds over time.  Read more cybersecurity stats at https://t.co/79O95EMzwA  #Cybersecurity #Cybersec https://t.co/d5nvy86IUr

Cybersecurity is one of those things most teams know matters, but it can rarely feel urgent in the moment.  Usually, there's always something competing for more attention: a system needing to be fixed, a tight deadline, or adoption of a tool that was supposed to make things easier.  So, security often becomes an afterthought.  The challenge is that as teams and systems scale, small gaps can turn into material risk.   Those gaps don’t just increase exposure; they make issues harder and more expensive to remediate once they surface.  By the time these gaps are visible, they're usually harder and more expensive to deal with.
